summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on March 11, 2025. A resident presented allegations of corruption regarding Administrator Bob Rose's contract, which the board addressed by noting no legal concerns have been raised by their attorneys. The board accepted firefighter resignations, approved a State Fire Marshal equipment grant, accepted a $45,732 donation from Day Heights Firefighters Association for fire gear, adopted resolutions for cannabis revenue allocation and the Clermont County 9-1-1 plan, and made several zoning board appointments including hiring John Langenheim as Planning and Zoning Director.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for February 26 through March 11, 2025
Accepted resignation of firefighter paramedic Chris Spinozzi effective March 4, 2025
Accepted full-time resignation of firefighter paramedic Adam Reed Daniel and changed classification to part-time at $23.50/hr effective March 28, 2025
Accepted Ohio State Fire Marshal Equipment Grant of $14,898.93
Accepted donation from Day Heights Firefighters Association of $45,732
Increased revenue in gifts and donations account 2111-801-0000 by $45,732
Increased appropriations in small tools and minor equipment account 2111-220-430-0000 by $45,732
Approved indigent burial of Walter N. Burnett at $903 to Advantage Cremation Care
Adopted Resolution R-003-2025 to accept and allocate cannabis revenue funds to general fund
Adopted Resolution R-004-2025 approving Clermont County 9-1-1 plan
Entered executive session at 6:23 PM per ORC 121.22 G1
Appointed Tammy Purcell as alternate on Zoning Commission with term ending March 11, 2027
Accepted resignation of John Langenheim from BZA effective March 4, 2025
Moved John Purcell from BZA alternate to BZA member with term ending December 31, 2025
action_items: Administrator Rose to process cannabis revenue funds into the general fund upon receipt of first check
Resolution R-004-2025 to be sent back to the 9-1-1 center (Jessica) after passage
Chief Myers/Deputy Chief Lynch to purchase 59 pairs of gloves, 13 pagers, and one car radio with Fire Marshal grant
Director Miles to process indigent burial reimbursement with Terry Shieldmeyer
Finalize hiring of John Langenheim as Planning and Zoning Director
key_amounts: $14,898.93 - Ohio State Fire Marshal Equipment Grant for gloves, pagers, and car radio
$45,732 - Donation from Day Heights Firefighters Association for fire gear
$903 - Indigent burial cost for Walter N. Burnett
$23.50/hr - Part-time rate for Adam Reed Daniel
$3.60 per $100 of adult-use cannabis sales goes to host community (3.6% of 10% excise tax)
$3,600 - Referenced past overtime pay issue with Bob Rose (historical)
notable_topics: Public complaint alleging Administrator Bob Rose's contract is illegal with formal complaints filed to Ohio Ethics Commission and Attorney General
Station 18 construction update - completion pushed to April 2026 due to cold weather and permitting
Acceptance of Day Heights Firefighters Association donation for fire gear
Ohio State Fire Marshal Equipment Grant acceptance
Cannabis revenue fund allocation resolution (from Ayer Wellness dispensary)
Clermont County 9-1-1 plan approval and funding distribution
Zoning Commission and BZA appointments including hiring of new Planning and Zoning Director
Firefighter resignations - Chris Spinozzi to City of Fairfield, Adam Reed Daniel to Hamilton Township
attendees: John Langenheim (new Planning and Zoning Director)
Terry Shieldmeyer (mentioned, assists with indigent burial reimbursement)
notes: A resident, Logan Bixler, delivered a detailed public speech alleging Administrator Bob Rose's 2025-2029 contract is illegal and citing past audit findings and evidence room issues. He stated he filed complaints with the Ohio Ethics Commission, Attorney General, Inspector General, State Auditor, and Clermont County Prosecutor, and directed attendees to his website loganbixler.com. Trustee Hodges responded diplomatically, noting no legal concerns have been communicated by attorneys. An executive session was held from approximately 6:23 PM to 7:07 PM regarding employment matters. The 2025 Salute to Leaders event was announced for April 9 at the Oasis recognizing Kevin Mayloff for bringing Homearama to Goshen Township. Senior citizens dinner announced for March 20 at the middle school.
title: BOT 3-11-25
